insights.title: Geopolitics, inflation, and the shifting dynamics of global bond markets
insights.summary: Geopolitical tensions have driven sharp volatility across global bond markets, with inflation concerns outweighing traditional safe-haven dynamics.

insights.title: CIO webinar: Examining the Iran conflict
insights.summary: Global markets have entered a period of heightened volatility following the latest escalation of tensions involving Iran, as energy prices and risk assets adjust to the prospect of a more sustained geopolitical conflict. Against this backdrop, our senior investment team assess the evolving macro risks, outline potential scenarios and discuss how portfolios are positioned to navigate near-term uncertainty while remaining focused on longer-term fundamentals.

insights.title: Iran conflict - implications for emerging markets
insights.summary: In March, tragic and concerning developments in the Middle East have added complexity to the narrative around emerging market (EM) equities. Conflict in the region has increased market volatility, prompted some de-risking across the EM complex and pushed oil prices higher, potentially affecting inflation. Here, we consider the potential impact of recent events on the broader EM asset class, individual markets, commodities, and the US dollar. It is important to note that events in Iran and the Mi

insights.title: Equities Monthly March 2026
insights.summary: While the focus in February was on the changing dynamics of the AI story, reflecting rotation away from mega cap tech and software, early March has been dominated by the conflict in the Middle East.

insights.title: Fixed Income Monthly - March 2026
insights.summary: The Fixed Income Monthly provides a forward-looking summary of the medium-term views from Fidelity’s Fixed Income team.

insights.title: Policy rates are easing, but money market strategies still matter
insights.summary: Amid increased uncertainty over the future path of policy rates, the role of money market strategies has come back into focus. With rates expected to ease only modestly from elevated levels, investors are increasingly focused on preserving income, liquidity and capital stability without taking too much duration risk. In this environment, our investment team highlight why money market strategies still offer an attractive solution for investors looking to navigate a higher-rate world.

insights.title: Navigating volatility amid conflict in the Middle East
insights.summary: Investors may better navigate the uncertainty by combining diversified core exposures with differentiated return streams and active asset allocation.

insights.title: Global Asset Allocation Insights - March 2026
insights.summary: Our Multi Asset team's views on which asset classes and markets are presenting the greatest opportunities and risks.

insights.title: Fidelity’s Kaleidoscope
insights.summary: Welcome to Fidelity’s Kaleidoscope, where we investigate some of the key challenges facing investors. Our aim is to lay out the facts and articulate potential implications, identifying investment approaches that can help investors capture any associated opportunities and manage related risks. Ultimately, our goal is to help our clients make the right investment choices in context of their individual circumstances and goals.

insights.title: European Real Estate Outlook 2026: Resilience as a source of return
insights.summary: European real estate is entering the next phase of the cycle following a volatile first half of the decade. Valuations have reset, macroeconomic conditions are stabilising, and long-term structural themes support a renewed focus on resilience. While the operating environment remains challenging, conditions increasingly favour selective investment and active asset management, with performance driven by asset selection as markets adjust to structural change.

insights.title: Insurance Outlook 2026: Navigating structural change
insights.summary: Global macro uncertainty continues to shape capital markets, while evolving regulation and heightened risk management demands are redefining how insurers allocate capital and build resilience. In this context, Global Head of Insurance Solutions Ghislain Perisse explores five key themes that are set to shape the investment landscape for insurers in 2026.
